repository.contacts module¶

Contacts Repository Module¶

This module contains the CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for managing contacts in the database, ensuring each user only accesses their own data.

repository.contacts.create_contact(body: ContactCreate, user: User, db: Session) → Contact[source]¶

Creates a new contact associated with a specific user.

Parameters:

  • body (ContactCreate) – The data for the new contact.

  • user (User) – The user who will own this contact.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

The newly created contact.

Return type:

Contact

repository.contacts.get_contact(contact_id: int, user: User, db: Session) → Contact[source]¶

Retrieves a single contact by its ID for a specific user.

Parameters:

  • contact_id (int) – The unique identifier of the contact.

  • user (User) – The owner of the contact.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

The contact object if found and owned by the user, otherwise None.

Return type:

Contact | None

repository.contacts.get_contacts(name: str, last_name: str, email: str, user: User, db: Session) → List[Contact][source]¶

Retrieves a list of contacts for a specific user with optional filters.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Optional filter by first name (case-insensitive partial match).

  • last_name (str) – Optional filter by last name (case-insensitive partial match).

  • email (str) – Optional filter by email address (case-insensitive partial match).

  • user (User) – The owner of the contacts.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

A list of contacts that match the criteria.

Return type:

List[Contact]

repository.contacts.remove_contact(contact_id: int, user: User, db: Session) → Contact | None[source]¶

Deletes a specific contact from the database for a given user.

Parameters:

  • contact_id (int) – The ID of the contact to be removed.

  • user (User) – The owner of the contact.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

The removed contact if successful, otherwise None.

Return type:

Contact | None

repository.contacts.update_contact(contact_id: int, body: ContactUpdate, user: User, db: Session) → Contact | None[source]¶

Updates an existing contact’s information for a specific user.

Only updates the fields provided in the body (exclude_unset=True).

Parameters:

  • contact_id (int) – The ID of the contact to update.

  • body (ContactUpdate) – The updated contact data.

  • user (User) – The owner of the contact.

  • db (Session) – The database session.

Returns:

The updated contact if found, otherwise None.

Return type:

Contact | None
